Can you confirm monthly active addressable users seen in Safari, Firefox and Edge?
Can you provide a list of publishers integrated with a first-party addressability solution?
Does the ID provide addressability in cookie-restricted browsers? And what is the step-by-step workflow for propagating the identifier?
Does your company tie consumer preferences (opt-in/opt-out) to your ID, or is the recording of these preferences dependent on legacy identifiers (such as third-party cookies)?
How is the ID able to associate CTV devices to users? And what is the step-by-step workflow for propagating the identifier?
Is the creation of identifiers dependent on users providing personally identifiable information (PII)?
Is the identifier constructed at user level?
Is the identifier present within Connected TV (CTV) supply?
What current SSP integrations do you have, by region and country?
What DSP integrations are there?

Which consent frameworks is the identifier integrated with or compatible with?
Will the ID provide addressability in iOS apps? And what is the step-by-step workflow for propagating the identifier?
Can you provide log-level or impression-level data for independent validation?
Does your measurement approach require personal data or user identification, and how is this handled under GDPR?
How do you distinguish between passive exposure and meaningful attention?
How do you ensure compliance with user consent frameworks (e.g. TCF) when collecting attention signals?
How do you validate the accuracy of your attention model against real-world outcomes or observed behavioural data?
How do your metrics align with emerging industry standards (e.g. IAB/MRC attention guidelines)?
Is your measurement approach independently audited or accredited? If so, by whom?
Is your solution cookieless, and how does it operate in privacy-restricted environments (e.g. Safari, CTV)?
To what extent does your methodology rely on panel-based data, and how representative is that panel?
What core attention metrics do you provide (e.g. attentive seconds, attention index, likelihood scores), and how are they defined?
What level of transparency do you provide into your modelling assumptions, training data, and weighting of signals?
What methodology do you use to measure attention (deterministic, probabilistic, or hybrid), and how do you differentiate between observed and modelled signals?
What specific signals (e.g. time-in-view, scroll velocity, audibility, gaze data) are used to calculate your attention metrics?
Do you support cookieless signals, contextual classification, or clean‑room integrations?
What is your data recency, refresh cadence, and expiry policy?
What kind of targeting data do you use to curate inventory?
Where and how is the targeting data collected?
Are IAB Europe Transparency & Consent Framework consent signals passed alongside the data?
Can you demonstrate the consent mechanism used and does it meet your data collection requirement?
Do you directly sell data as a revenue stream or do you simply enable the sale of data between buyers and sellers?

Is a margin applied to third-party data?
Is the data declared (provided directly by users), observed (based on behaviour), or modelled (extrapolated)?

What identifiers are present in the data (e.g. cookies, mobile advertising IDs, alternative identifiers)?
